name: Home
title: Home
description: Homepage that lets guests and signed-in customers log in or sign up, see dynamic featured items and store information, view a current pickup order summary, and quickly navigate to ordering, stores, rewards, account dashboard, and order history.

core_features: Load dynamic homepage content (featured products, promotions, and favorite or suggested store summary) using getHomePageContent on page load.
Display authentication controls in the header, providing log in and sign up flows that call loginWithEmailPassword and signupAccount, and a log out action that calls logoutCurrentAccount.
Show a compact current order summary widget using getCurrentOrderSummary, including item count, amounts, and pickup store/time when an active order exists.
For logged-in users, load account overview data and quick links using getAccountDashboardSummary and expose shortcuts such as Account dashboard access and a View Past Orders card.
Provide a primary Order Pickup call-to-action that navigates to menu.html for starting or continuing a pickup order.
Provide a store search form that navigates to stores.html with a zip query parameter based on user input.
Provide a Bakery Rewards shortcut that navigates to rewards.html with category=bakery, and allow rewards-related promotions on the homepage to link to the same destination when configured.
Allow navigation to the account dashboard from the header or account name/avatar, passing source=home in the URL.
user_workflows:
Guest logs in from the homepage:
1. Guest opens index.html (no required URL parameters).
2. The homepage-init-controller component calls getHomePageContent and getCurrentOrderSummary.
3. Header shows generic Account / Log In / Sign Up controls because getHomePageContent.isLoggedIn is false.
4. Guest clicks the Log In control in the header; header-global-nav sets the URL parameter auth=login and opens the login-modal.
5. Guest enters email and password and submits the login form; login-modal calls loginWithEmailPassword with those values.
6. If the response.success is true and response.isLoggedIn is true, the page updates in-memory page state with account data from response.account, closes the login-modal, and the homepage-init-controller re-calls getHomePageContent and getAccountDashboardSummary so header-global-nav, store-summary-card, and account-quicklinks-panel rerender with logged-in information.
7. The URL is updated (e.g., using history.replaceState) to remove auth=login to return to a clean index.html state.

Guest signs up then starts a pickup order:
1. Guest opens index.html.
2. Guest clicks Sign Up in the header; header-global-nav sets auth=signup in the URL and opens signup-modal.
3. Guest fills fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, and accepts terms; signup-modal validates locally and then calls signupAccount.
4. If signupAccount returns success=true, signup-modal treats the user as logged in, reads accountDashboard from the response, and passes this to homepage-init-controller.
5. homepage-init-controller updates page state to logged-in, triggers a refresh of getHomePageContent if needed, and populates account-quicklinks-panel using accountDashboard data until getAccountDashboardSummary is optionally fetched to fully sync.
6. Guest clicks the primary Order Pickup call-to-action; order-pickup-cta navigates to menu.html (no additional URL parameters) where the user can configure a pickup order.

Logged-in user checks account overview and views past orders:
1. Logged-in user opens index.html; getHomePageContent returns isLoggedIn=true.
2. homepage-init-controller calls getAccountDashboardSummary to retrieve fullName, email, loyaltyPointsBalance, loyaltyTier, favoriteStore, and quickLinks.
3. account-summary-panel displays the user greeting and loyalty information; account-quicklinks-panel renders shortcuts based on quickLinks.
4. The panel surfaces a dedicated View Past Orders shortcut; when clicked, account-orders-shortcut navigates to account_orders.html?sortBy=date_desc.
5. If the user clicks their account name/avatar or the Account link in the header, header-global-nav navigates to account_dashboard.html?source=home.

User searches for a store from the homepage:
1. User loads index.html; store-search-form is visible.
2. User enters a ZIP code into the search field.
3. On submit, store-search-form validates the ZIP input format and then navigates the browser to stores.html?zip={enteredZip}.
4. The stores page uses the zip parameter for its own logic; no store search is performed directly on the Home page beyond redirection.

User reviews current pickup order from the homepage:
1. User with an active draft or placed order returns to index.html.
2. homepage-init-controller calls getCurrentOrderSummary; this returns hasActiveOrder=true with orderId, itemCount, subtotalAmount, totalAmount, pickupStore, and pickupDateTime.
3. current-order-summary-widget renders a compact summary in a persistent area of the page and a Continue Order button.
4. When the user clicks Continue Order, current-order-summary-widget navigates to menu.html (optionally including the current orderId in the URL if menu.html supports it), allowing the user to modify or review the order.

User logs out from the homepage:
1. Logged-in user opens index.html; header-global-nav shows the account display with a logout action.
2. User opens the account menu in header-global-nav and clicks Log Out.
3. header-global-nav calls logoutCurrentAccount.
4. If logoutCurrentAccount.success is true, homepage-init-controller clears in-memory account state, re-runs getHomePageContent and getCurrentOrderSummary (which now return guest context), and redirects to index.html?loggedOut=1.
5. A logout-confirmation-banner component (within this page) checks the loggedOut URL parameter and, if present, displays a transient confirmation message; after display, the component can remove loggedOut from the URL using history.replaceState.
interactions:
Header navigation interactions:
- Clicking the site logo or Home link triggers navigation to index.html (no extra parameters).
- Clicking the Order Pickup header link triggers navigation to menu.html.
- Clicking the Stores header link triggers navigation to stores.html without parameters; the stores page may handle its own default state.
- Clicking the Account header link when not logged in opens login-modal and sets auth=login; when logged in, it navigates to account_dashboard.html?source=home.
- Clicking the visible account name/avatar when logged in navigates to account_dashboard.html?source=home.
- Clicking Log In in the header opens login-modal and sets auth=login in the URL.
- Clicking Sign Up in the header opens signup-modal and sets auth=signup in the URL.
- Clicking Log Out in the header invokes logoutCurrentAccount and, on success, redirects to index.html?loggedOut=1.

Authentication form interactions:
- login-modal onSubmit: reads email and password from inputs, calls loginWithEmailPassword, and, on success, updates page state to logged-in, closes the modal, and removes auth from the URL; on failure, displays response.message as an inline error while keeping the modal open.
- login-modal onClose: closes the modal and removes the auth URL parameter if its value is login.
- signup-modal onSubmit: reads fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, and terms acceptance; validates confirmPassword and acceptTerms locally; then calls signupAccount. On success, it updates page state to logged-in using accountDashboard, closes the modal, and removes auth from the URL; on failure, it surfaces response.message.
- signup-modal onClose: closes the modal and removes auth from the URL if its value is signup.

Homepage content interactions:
- featured-products-list: clicking a featured product item opens menu.html, allowing the user to start or continue ordering that product (the menu page determines the exact behavior).
- promotions-list: clicking a promotion whose behavior is configured as navigational may either keep the user on Home or redirect to another page; for promotions that represent rewards (such as a bakery-focused promotion), the click handler navigates to rewards.html?category=bakery.
- bakery-rewards-shortcut: clicking this shortcut always navigates to rewards.html?category=bakery.

Store-related interactions:
- store-search-form onSubmit: prevents default form submission, validates the ZIP code, then navigates to stores.html?zip={encodedZip}.
- store-summary-card: clicking the Change store link navigates to stores.html without parameters; clicking the Order Pickup button navigates to menu.html, using the store implied by storeSummary as context that the menu page can use via its own interfaces.

Account and orders interactions:
- account-summary-panel: clicking a View Account button navigates to account_dashboard.html?source=home.
- account-quicklinks-panel: renders quickLinks items from getAccountDashboardSummary. For a quick link whose key corresponds to order history (for example, a key that includes 'orders'), clicking that tile navigates to account_orders.html?sortBy=date_desc; other keys can map to their respective destinations as defined by global navigation rules.
- account-orders-shortcut: when present, clicking this explicit View Past Orders card always navigates to account_orders.html?sortBy=date_desc.

Current order interactions:
- current-order-summary-widget: when hasActiveOrder is true, clicking the main widget or an explicit Continue Order button navigates to menu.html (optionally appending orderId as a URL parameter if supported downstream). If hasActiveOrder is false, the widget either remains hidden or displays a Start an Order button that navigates to menu.html.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The Home page does not require any incoming URL parameters for data loading; getHomePageContent, getCurrentOrderSummary, and getAccountDashboardSummary are all called without parameters and return context-specific data (guest vs logged-in) based on the server-side session.

However, URL parameters are used to control client-side state and drive navigation:
- auth parameter: When the URL includes auth=login, homepage-init-controller opens login-modal on page load. When auth=signup, it opens signup-modal. These parameters are set by header-global-nav when the user clicks the Log In or Sign Up controls and are removed after the modal is closed or authentication succeeds, using history.replaceState.
- loggedOut parameter: When the page is loaded with loggedOut=1, logout-confirmation-banner displays a transient confirmation message. After showing the message, it removes loggedOut from the URL via history.replaceState so subsequent reloads do not repeat the banner.
- Outgoing parameters to other pages: The Home page constructs outgoing URLs using user input or fixed routing rules:
  • stores.html?zip={zipFromStoreSearchForm} when the user submits store-search-form.
  • account_dashboard.html?source=home when the user clicks the Account link or account avatar while logged in.
  • rewards.html?category=bakery when the user clicks bakery-rewards-shortcut or a rewards-configured promotion.
  • account_orders.html?sortBy=date_desc when the user clicks View Past Orders on account-orders-shortcut or the relevant quick link.
  • menu.html (with no required query parameters) when the user clicks Order Pickup in the header, order-pickup-cta, featured-products-list items, or current-order-summary-widget.

Page state is maintained in memory for the duration of the session in the browser tab and is always derived from the latest responses of loginWithEmailPassword, signupAccount, getHomePageContent, getCurrentOrderSummary, and getAccountDashboardSummary. No state is stored in localStorage or other persistent client storage; instead, the application re-derives state by re-invoking these interfaces on reload or navigation back to index.html.

components.functionality: Initializes the Home page by calling getHomePageContent and getCurrentOrderSummary on load, optionally calling getAccountDashboardSummary when isLoggedIn is true, and coordinating updates to other components based on these responses and URL parameters.

Displays global navigation links (Home, Order Pickup, Stores, Account), shows login/signup controls or the logged-in account name/avatar based on isLoggedIn, exposes a logout action, and triggers appropriate modals or page navigations.
Provides an email/password login form; calls loginWithEmailPassword on submit, handles success and error responses, and updates the homepage state to logged-in.

Provides a fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, and terms acceptance registration form; calls signupAccount on submit, initializes logged-in state using accountDashboard, and closes itself on success.
Displays a transient logout confirmation message when the loggedOut=1 URL parameter is present and then removes that parameter from the URL.
Captures a ZIP code from the user and, on submit, navigates to stores.html with the zip parameter set to the entered value.
Displays either the favoriteStore or suggestedStore from getHomePageContent.storeSummary, including open status if available, and provides shortcuts to change store or start an order.
Renders a list of featuredProducts from getHomePageContent with basic details and a Start Order interaction for each item that navigates to menu.html.

Displays promotions from getHomePageContent.promotions and, when a promotion is configured as rewards-related, routes clicks to rewards.html with category=bakery; otherwise, promotions can be informational or link to other appropriate destinations.
Provides a dedicated shortcut from the Home page to the rewards page filtered by bakery rewards, navigating to rewards.html?category=bakery when clicked.
Acts as the primary Order Pickup call-to-action on the Home page, navigating directly to menu.html when clicked.

Shows a compact summary of the current order using getCurrentOrderSummary when hasActiveOrder is true, including item count, monetary amounts, pickup store name, and pickup time, with a Continue Order action that navigates to menu.html.

Displays a concise account overview for logged-in users using getAccountDashboardSummary, including fullName, email, loyaltyPointsBalance, loyaltyTier, and favoriteStore, plus a View Account action that navigates to account_dashboard.html?source=home.

Renders quickLinks from getAccountDashboardSummary as clickable tiles; for links that correspond to order history, clicking navigates to account_orders.html?sortBy=date_desc; other links route to their respective destinations as defined by the app routing rules.
Provides an explicit View Past Orders shortcut card on the Home page that navigates to account_orders.html?sortBy=date_desc when clicked, independent of quickLinks.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column grid with a centered content column constrained to a fixed max-width (~420–480px). Header uses a three-part horizontal layout (left, center, right) aligned to the same page margins.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a full-width canvas with a thin, three-part global header at the top and a single, centered content column beneath it. The header spans the full width, with logo/navigation on the left, the store-search-form sitting in the center as a compact 'Find a location' control, and account/order links on the right. Below the header (and any banner) all main components are stacked vertically inside a narrow central column (max-width ~420–480px), centered on the page. Within this column, sections follow a strict top-to-bottom order: account area (account-summary-panel and account-quicklinks-panel / account-orders-shortcut for signed-in users), local store and pickup section (store-summary-card, order-pickup-cta, current-order-summary-widget), then discovery and marketing content (featured-products-list, promotions-list, bakery-rewards-shortcut). Login-modal and signup-modal appear only as centered overlays on top of this layout when triggered.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a full-width canvas with a thin, three-part global header at the top and a single, centered content column beneath it. The header spans the full width, with logo/navigation on the left, the store-search-form sitting in the center as a compact 'Find a location' control, and account/order links on the right. Below the header (and any banner) all main components are stacked vertically inside a narrow central column (max-width ~420–480px), centered on the page. Within this column, sections follow a strict top-to-bottom order: account area (account-summary-panel and account-quicklinks-panel / account-orders-shortcut for signed-in users), local store and pickup section (store-summary-card, order-pickup-cta, current-order-summary-widget), then discovery and marketing content (featured-products-list, promotions-list, bakery-rewards-shortcut). Login-modal and signup-modal appear only as centered overlays on top of this layout when triggered.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies a slim band at the top, roughly 10–15% of the viewport height. Immediately beneath, the logout-confirmation-banner, when present, is an even thinner strip. The remaining 80–85% of vertical space is dominated by the central column, which floats in the middle of the page with generous top and bottom padding. Each card or widget inside this column uses the full column width with consistent internal padding. The primary CTAs (order-pickup-cta, and the main buttons inside login-modal/signup-modal) span nearly the full column width and visually anchor their sections. Lower-priority links and descriptive text are compact and use tighter spacing, staying at the bottom of their respective blocks. Modals use the same column width but are vertically centered within a semi-opaque full-viewport overlay, temporarily becoming the dominant element.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout is intentionally low-density and minimalist. Each section within the central column contains only a small number of elements: a concise heading or label, short descriptive text, and a single primary action where needed. No multi-column layouts are used inside the main content; lists like featured-products-list and promotions-list are presented as vertically stacked cards with comfortable gaps. Inputs, buttons, and links have generous padding and whitespace around them. Ancillary items like 'Forgot password?' or secondary navigation shortcuts are rendered in smaller text with tighter vertical spacing, clearly subordinate to the primary CTA in each section.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts at the top-left logo and nav in the header, then naturally to the centered store-search-form (echoing the 'Find a location' pattern from the mockup). From there, the eye drops into the central column: first encountering the account state (either the account-summary-panel for signed-in users or a sign-in trigger leading to the login-modal), then moving down to the local store and pickup CTA (store-summary-card followed by the large order-pickup-cta button). After that, attention flows to the current-order-summary-widget, then down into discovery content (featured products and promotions), and finally to rewards and account shortcuts. When login-modal or signup-modal is open, the visual flow mirrors the original mockup: headline, supporting text, stacked form fields, large blue primary button, then subtle secondary links beneath.

layout.overall_description: The homepage appears as a clean, modern canvas with a slim, full-width header pinned to the top. The header is divided into three horizontal regions: on the left sits the coffee shop logo and a simple navigation trigger; in the center, a pill-shaped store-search-form serves as a focused 'Find a location' control; on the right, compact links to rewards, orders, and account actions line up along the same horizontal axis. Beneath the header, optionally a thin logout-confirmation-banner spans edge to edge, then the main content begins: a single, narrow column centered on the page with wide white margins on both sides. This column feels calm and uncluttered, with each card or section separated by generous vertical spacing. At the top of the column, a compact account area acknowledges returning customers with a summary and quick links, or, for guests, provides clear triggers to sign in or sign up via the modals. Immediately below, the local store and pickup section presents the current store summary, followed by a bold, full-width brand-blue order-pickup-cta button that anchors this middle band of the page. Under that, a simple current-order-summary-widget previews any active pickup order in a lightly outlined card. The page then transitions smoothly into discovery content: a featured-products-list and promotions-list, each with vertically stacked item cards, followed by a bakery-rewards-shortcut and account-orders-shortcut as compact navigation cards. Everything is left-aligned within the column, but visually centered on the page as a whole. When a login or signup flow is triggered, the background subtly dims and a centered modal, matching the central column’s width, appears with a heading, short copy, stacked form fields, and a large blue button, echoing the clean, vertical login experience of the original mockup.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This controller component does not render any visible UI. It sits conceptually at the top of the page structure, driving which sections of the central column are shown (for example, whether account-summary-panel or login/signup triggers are visible, and whether current-order-summary-widget is present). It occupies no space in the visual layout and does not affect the grid directly.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; purely logical. It has no spatial footprint and does not participate in document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global navigation header is a slim, full-width bar anchored to the top of the viewport. It follows a three-part horizontal grid aligned to the page margins. The content within the header is constrained to a wider max-width (for example ~960–1200px), centered on the screen. Within this area, the left third contains the brand logo and a compact 'Home' or back navigation aligned to the left margin. The center third houses the store-search-form component as a pill-shaped control, aligned to the center of the header. The right third contains horizontally arranged, text-only links or icons for Rewards, Orders, and Account, right-aligned. Vertically, the header height is modest (about 10–12% of the viewport height or 56–72px on desktop), with equal padding above and below its contents to maintain the minimalist feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sticky at the top of the viewport so it remains visible on scroll; it pushes the rest of the content down and never overlaps the central column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When activated, the login-modal overlays the entire viewport with a semi-transparent white or darkened scrim. In the center of the screen, a card appears that mirrors the central column width (max-width around 420–480px) and is horizontally centered. The card is vertically centered on large screens but allows some top and bottom breathing room so it never touches the edges. Inside the card, elements are stacked top-to-bottom: a bold 'Welcome back' or similar heading at the top, then a short line of instructional text. Beneath that, the form fields are arranged in a vertical line: email label and input spanning nearly the full card width, then a comfortable gap followed by the password label and input. Below the inputs, a full-width brand-blue 'Sign In' button sits as the most visually prominent element. Under the button, a single left-aligned 'Forgot password?' link appears with tighter vertical spacing, followed by a thin horizontal rule and a final line of text with an inline 'Sign Up' link in smaller type. All content is left-aligned within the modal card, but the card itself is centered on the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position overlay that sits above all other components, disables background scrolling, and centers itself within the viewport. It can scroll internally on very small screens if content exceeds height.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The signup-modal appears and behaves similarly to the login-modal, sharing the same full-viewport scrim and centered card with a max-width around 420–480px. The card is horizontally centered and vertically centered with generous margin to the top and bottom of the viewport. Inside, content is stacked in a clean vertical column: a bold 'Create account' style heading at the top, a short explanatory sentence beneath, followed by multiple form fields (such as name, email, password) each arranged in label–input pairs stacked top-to-bottom. Each input spans almost the full card width, with consistent vertical spacing between them. A full-width brand-blue 'Sign Up' button anchors the form just below the last input. Below the button, optional secondary text (such as 'Already have an account? Sign In') appears in smaller type with the action link inline. As with the login-modal, text and controls are left-aligned inside the card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position overlay above all content with a dimming scrim, locking page scroll. The card can scroll internally if content extends beyond available height, maintaining top and bottom padding.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The logout-confirmation-banner sits directly below the header-global-nav, spanning the full width of the page from left to right. It is a thin horizontal bar, significantly shorter than the header (roughly 40–56px tall), with text centered vertically within it. The content (e.g., 'You have been signed out') is centered horizontally within the same max-width container as the header content and central column alignment, but the background color of the banner provides a subtle contrast (for example, a light blue or tinted background) so it is noticeable but not overwhelming. There is minimal vertical padding inside the banner to keep it compact, reflecting its secondary priority.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: In normal flow beneath the sticky header. When visible, it pushes the central content column down; it does not overlay other content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The store-search-form manifests visually in the center section of the header-global-nav. It appears as a horizontally oriented pill or rounded rectangle input that spans roughly 60% of the header’s central third, maintaining comfortable left and right padding so it does not touch adjacent nav elements. The input is centered horizontally within this middle region and vertically aligned within the header’s height. Inside the pill, an icon and placeholder text (e.g., 'Find a location') are aligned left, with ample internal padding to keep the form clean and touch-friendly. On narrow screens, the pill may reduce to about 70–80% of the viewport width but stays visually centered under the logo when the header stacks.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Lives inside the header layout; its horizontal size responds within the header’s central area but it does not scroll independently. As the header is sticky, this control remains visible at the top during page scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The store-summary-card is the first substantial card in the central content column beneath the header and any logout-confirmation-banner, unless an account-summary-panel appears above it for signed-in users. It is centered within the page, constrained to the narrow column width (max 420–480px), and uses a light outline or subtle shadow with rounded corners. Inside, content is left-aligned and stacked: the store name appears at the top in a slightly larger, bold font; a line or two of secondary details (address, distance, hours) follow with moderate spacing. If there is an inline link like 'Change store', it sits beneath the details in smaller text. Vertical padding inside the card is generous, but the overall card height remains compact relative to the order-pickup-cta that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Placed in the standard vertical flow of the central column, with equal top and bottom margins separating it from neighboring components. It expands vertically based on the amount of store information but always respects the column width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured-products-list appears in the central column below the current-order-summary-widget, separated by a generous vertical gap. It occupies the full width of the narrow column and is left-aligned. At the top of this section, a simple heading such as 'Featured this week' appears, followed by a short gap and then a vertical stack of product items. Each product item is presented as a full-width mini-card or row: product name and a short description are left-aligned, with a price or small thumbnail aligned to the right inside each item row if needed. Items are separated by consistent vertical spacing (for example 16–24px) to maintain the low-density feel. The section height grows as more items are added, but the single-column structure and generous spacing keep it from feeling crowded.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow section within the central column. It stretches vertically according to content length and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The promotions-list sits directly beneath the featured-products-list, separated by a modest but noticeable vertical gap. It uses the same central column width and left alignment. A concise section title such as 'Promotions' appears at the top, followed by one or more promotion cards stacked vertically. Each promotion card spans the full width of the column, with a light border or background tint to differentiate it from surrounding white space. Inside each card, a bold promo title sits at the top, with a short line of descriptive text beneath and, optionally, a subtle text link like 'Learn more' at the bottom. Vertical padding in each card is slightly more generous than a plain list item, but still restrained to preserve simplicity.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the normal vertical flow within the central column; it expands based on the number of promotions and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The bakery-rewards-shortcut appears in the central column just below the promotions-list, occupying the full width of the narrow column. It is rendered as a single navigation card with a subtle outline or tinted background that distinguishes it but keeps it visually light. The card height is compact; inside, an icon or small graphic may appear on the left, with a brief title like 'Bakery Rewards' and a one-line description stacked to its right, all left-aligned. The entire card behaves like a clickable area, with comfortable internal padding but less vertical height than a primary CTA button. It maintains the same left margin as all other components, keeping the single-column alignment consistent.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: In-flow navigation card within the central column. It does not stick or float; it simply scrolls with the rest of the page content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The order-pickup-cta is the main action element in the central column and appears immediately beneath the store-summary-card, separated by a comfortable gap. It is a large, full-width brand-blue button that spans nearly the entire width of the central column (leaving only small horizontal padding at the edges). The button height is generous, similar to the 'Sign In' button in the mockup, and its label text is centered both horizontally and vertically. There is slightly more vertical space above the button than below, emphasizing its connection to the store-summary-card while still allowing the eye to flow downward. This button visually anchors the central portion of the page and is the most prominent interactive element outside of any modals.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow component within the central column. It does not stick or float, but its size, color, and position make it visually dominant as users scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The current-order-summary-widget sits directly below the order-pickup-cta in the central column, with a moderate vertical gap between them. It is presented as a single card spanning the full column width, with a subtle border or soft shadow and rounded corners matching the store-summary-card style. Inside, content is arranged vertically: a small heading like 'Current pickup order' at the top, then a list of one to three items with concise names and quantities, and finally a brief order footer (e.g., total price or pickup time) at the bottom. Items may use a simple two-column row layout inside the card (item name on the left, price on the right), but the card itself never breaks the single-column page structure. Vertical spacing between lines is comfortable but not excessive, keeping the card compact and scannable beneath the large CTA button.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: In normal document flow beneath the order CTA. The widget grows vertically with the number of items and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: For signed-in users, the account-summary-panel appears at the very top of the central content column, directly below the header (or below the logout-confirmation-banner when present) and above the store-summary-card. It uses the same narrow column width (max 420–480px) and is centered on the page. The panel is a low-profile card with a light background or border. Inside, content is left-aligned: a greeting such as 'Welcome, [Name]' at the top, followed by a line or two of key account information (rewards balance, available offers). A small, right-aligned link like 'View account' may appear within the panel, but the layout remains strictly single-column. Vertical padding is generous, but the overall panel height is moderate so it does not overshadow the order-pickup-cta further down.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow card at the top of the central column. When the user is not signed in, this panel is omitted entirely, allowing the store-summary-card to shift up into its place.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The account-quicklinks-panel appears directly beneath the account-summary-panel for signed-in users, separated by a modest vertical gap while still near the top of the central column. It occupies the full width of the narrow column. Inside, a simple heading such as 'Quick links' appears at the top, with a vertical list of two to four text links or compact pill buttons beneath (e.g., 'Account settings', 'Payment methods', 'Rewards'). Each link is left-aligned and spans the width of the panel, but vertical spacing between links is tight enough to keep the panel from feeling tall. This panel visually groups account-related actions without overwhelming the user, serving as a lighter-weight navigational block compared to the bold order-pickup-cta.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the central column’s normal flow, appearing only for signed-in users. It expands vertically based on the number of quick links and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The account-orders-shortcut is a dedicated navigation card focused on order history. It appears in the central column just below the account-quicklinks-panel for signed-in users, or directly below other account-related content if quicklinks are not present. The card spans the full column width and has a slight visual emphasis (e.g., a subtle accent border or icon) so it stands out from simple text links but remains less prominent than the main order-pickup-cta. Inside, a title like 'Order history' is left-aligned at the top, perhaps with a short supporting line such as 'View past orders'. The card height is moderate, with comfortable internal padding but a compact vertical footprint.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: In-flow navigation card within the central column. It is only visible for users with an account context and scrolls normally with the rest of the content.

name: Stores Search & Results
title: Stores
description: Search for nearby coffee shop locations by ZIP code or current location, view store details summaries, and select a store to view its detail page or start an order, with optional account login and current order summary.

core_features: Search for stores by ZIP code using the searchStoresByZip interface and show results on the same page.
Search for stores near the user’s current coordinates using the searchStoresByCoordinates interface when the user opts to use current location.
Display a list of store search results including distance, hours summary, open/closed state, and whether mobile ordering is available.
Allow navigation from a selected store to store_detail.html with the store ID and the current ZIP search value passed as URL parameters.
Allow starting an order from a selected store by navigating to menu.html with the pickupStoreId URL parameter.
Provide login, logout, and signup flows via loginWithEmailPassword, logoutCurrentAccount, and signupAccount, enriching the page with account context such as favorite store and loyalty status.
Display a compact current order summary using getCurrentOrderSummary in the header or a dedicated widget, and allow navigation to the active order’s menu context if appropriate.
user_workflows: Workflow: Search stores by ZIP code. Steps: 1) User navigates to stores.html (with or without a zip parameter). 2) If a zip URL parameter is present, the page-init-controller reads it, pre-populates the ZIP input, and automatically calls searchStoresByZip with that value. 3) The stores-results-list component displays the returned stores. 4) If no zip parameter is present, the ZIP input is empty; the user types a ZIP code and submits the search form. 5) On submit, the stores-search-form component calls searchStoresByZip with the entered ZIP, updates the results list, and updates the browser URL to include the zip parameter.

Workflow: Search stores by current location. Steps: 1) User clicks a 'Use Current Location' control inside the stores-search-form. 2) The browser requests geolocation permission and, on success, provides latitude and longitude. 3) The stores-search-form component calls searchStoresByCoordinates with those coordinates. 4) The stores-results-list component displays the returned stores. 5) The URL zip parameter remains unchanged (or absent) because this search is coordinate-based; the results are treated as a separate search mode within the same page.

Workflow: View store details from search results. Steps: 1) User completes a ZIP or coordinate search and sees store cards in the stores-results-list. 2) For a specific store, the user clicks the store name or a 'View Details' interaction in the store-result-card component. 3) The store-result-card reads the current ZIP input value (if any) from the stores-search-form. 4) The component navigates to store_detail.html with URL parameters id set to the selected store’s id and fromZip set to the current ZIP input value (or omitted if no ZIP search is active).

Workflow: Start an order pickup from a store. Steps: 1) User completes a search and finds a desired store in the stores-results-list. 2) The user clicks an 'Order Pickup' interaction on that store’s store-result-card. 3) The store-result-card navigates to menu.html with the pickupStoreId parameter set to that store’s id. 4) The menu page uses pickupStoreId to initialize the order context for pickup at the chosen store.

Workflow: Log in from the Stores page. Steps: 1) User clicks a 'Log In' action in the header-account-controls or account-auth-panel. 2) The account-auth-panel shows a login form. 3) User enters email and password and submits. 4) The account-auth-panel calls loginWithEmailPassword with the entered credentials. 5) On success, the component updates in-page account state with returned account data (fullName, email, loyaltyPointsBalance, loyaltyTier, favoriteStore). 6) The header-account-controls updates to show the logged-in state. 7) Optionally, if a ZIP is currently in the search input, the stores-search-form re-runs searchStoresByZip so that the new results can include the isFavorite flag and reflect the favorite store.

Workflow: Sign up for a new account from the Stores page. Steps: 1) User selects a 'Create Account' action in the header-account-controls or account-auth-panel. 2) The account-auth-panel shows a signup form requesting fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, and a checkbox for acceptTerms. 3) User fills in the fields and submits. 4) The component calls signupAccount with the provided values. 5) On success, it uses the returned accountDashboard context to set in-page account state and updates header-account-controls to show the authenticated state. 6) If the user already has a ZIP-based search visible, the stores-search-form can optionally re-run searchStoresByZip to re-annotate the results using the new account’s favorite store, if provided.

Workflow: Logout from the Stores page. Steps: 1) While logged in, the header-account-controls shows a 'Log Out' interaction. 2) User clicks 'Log Out'. 3) The logout-button component calls logoutCurrentAccount. 4) On success, account-related UI elements (header-account-controls, account-auth-panel) update to a logged-out state. 5) Optionally, the stores-search-form may re-run the last ZIP search to remove isFavorite markings tied to the previous account’s favorite store.

Workflow: View current order summary from the Stores page. Steps: 1) On page load, the page-init-controller calls getCurrentOrderSummary. 2) The order-summary-widget displays hasActiveOrder, itemCount, subtotalAmount, totalAmount, pickupStore, and pickupDateTime if an active order exists. 3) If the user clicks the order-summary-widget, the component navigates to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to the pickupStore.id in the summary, allowing the user to continue modifying or reviewing the active order in the context of that store.
interactions: Typing a ZIP code into the ZIP input field and submitting the stores-search-form triggers the searchStoresByZip interface with the current input value and updates the results and URL zip parameter.

Clicking the 'Use Current Location' interaction triggers a geolocation request in the browser; on success, the stores-search-form calls searchStoresByCoordinates with the returned latitude and longitude and updates the stores-results-list.

Clicking on a store name or 'View Details' interaction in a store-result-card triggers navigation to store_detail.html with id set to that store’s id and fromZip set to the current ZIP input value (if any).
Clicking the 'Order Pickup' interaction within a store-result-card navigates to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to that store’s id.

Submitting the login form inside account-auth-panel triggers loginWithEmailPassword with the email and password fields; based on the returned success flag, the component either shows an error or updates account UI (header-account-controls) and optionally refreshes store search results.

Submitting the signup form inside account-auth-panel triggers signupAccount with fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, and acceptTerms; on success, the page updates to reflect a logged-in state and may refresh store search results to show favorite store state.
Clicking the 'Log Out' interaction in logout-button triggers logoutCurrentAccount, then resets account-related UI components to a logged-out state and may refresh store search results.

Loading the page triggers the page-init-controller to read the zip URL parameter (if present), call searchStoresByZip with that ZIP, and call getCurrentOrderSummary to populate the order-summary-widget.
Clicking the order-summary-widget when hasActiveOrder is true triggers navigation to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to the summary.pickupStore.id.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is driven by the URL query parameters and the results of the assigned interfaces. On initial load, the page-init-controller reads the zip URL parameter (if present). If zip is present and non-empty, it pre-fills the ZIP input in the stores-search-form with that value and immediately calls searchStoresByZip using that ZIP, populating the stores-results-list. If zip is absent, the search input starts empty and no search is executed until the user submits a search or uses current location. Whenever the user performs a ZIP-based search via the stores-search-form, the page updates the browser URL to include or replace the zip parameter with the new value so that the search can be restored when the user reloads or shares the URL. Coordinate-based searches using searchStoresByCoordinates do not modify the zip parameter; the current ZIP input and URL continue to reflect the last explicit ZIP search (if any), but the in-memory store list temporarily reflects the coordinate-based search. When navigating to store_detail.html, the page passes the store id from the selected result and the current ZIP input value as fromZip so the detail page can reconstruct or display the originating search context. When navigating to menu.html from either a store-result-card or the order-summary-widget, the page sets pickupStoreId in the URL based on the chosen store or the active order’s pickupStore. No localStorage or similar browser storage is used for state persistence; all restorable state is encoded in URL parameters (such as zip, id, fromZip, and pickupStoreId) and in the immediate responses from the assigned interfaces.

components.functionality: On page load, initializes the Stores page by reading URL parameters, triggering initial searches, and fetching the current order summary. If a zip parameter exists, it triggers a ZIP-based store search. It also coordinates updates to other components when account or order state changes.
data_binding: AccountProfile (derived from loginWithEmailPassword or signupAccount responses)
event_handlers: onLoad: read URL param zip; if present, call searchStoresByZip(zip) and pass results to stores-results-list; also call getCurrentOrderSummary() and pass result to order-summary-widget.
onAccountLoginSuccess: receive account from account-auth-panel and notify stores-search-form and stores-results-list so they can update favorite store indicators.
onAccountLogoutSuccess: notify stores-search-form and stores-results-list to clear favorite store indicators and, if desired, re-run the last ZIP-based search.
onUrlChange: if the zip parameter changes due to user search, re-run searchStoresByZip with the new zip value.

components.functionality: Provides inputs and controls for searching stores by ZIP code or by current location. Maintains the current search input and notifies the results list when new results are available.
data_binding: URL param: zip (used to pre-populate the ZIP input)
AccountProfile.favoriteStore (from loginWithEmailPassword or signupAccount) to optionally highlight a matching store in results
event_handlers: onSubmitZipSearch: validate the ZIP input and call searchStoresByZip(zipCode); on success, update the browser URL zip parameter and pass the resulting store array to stores-results-list.

onUseCurrentLocationClick: request browser geolocation; on success, call searchStoresByCoordinates(latitude, longitude); on success, pass the resulting store array to stores-results-list without modifying the zip URL parameter.
onZipInputChange: update in-memory ZIP value so it can be used when navigating to store_detail.html as fromZip.
onAccountContextUpdate: when notified of account login or signup, optionally re-run searchStoresByZip using the current ZIP to refresh results with isFavorite flags.

components.functionality: Displays a list of store search results received from the search interfaces and manages selection of a specific store. For each store, it renders a store-result-card with actions for viewing details and starting an order.
data_binding: AccountProfile.favoriteStore.id (used to mark a result as favorite when matching a store id)
Derived state: current search mode (ZIP-based or coordinate-based) from stores-search-form
event_handlers: onResultsUpdate: when new results are provided by stores-search-form, re-render the list of store-result-card components.
onStoreCardAction: handle events coming from individual store-result-card components (view details or order pickup) and delegate navigation actions.
onAccountContextUpdate: update highlighting of any store that matches the current favoriteStore id when account state changes.

components.functionality: Represents a single store entry in the results list. Shows store attributes (name, address, distance, hoursSummary, isOpenNow, canMobileOrder, favorite status) and provides actions to view details or start an order pickup at this store.
data_binding: AccountProfile.favoriteStore.id or isFavorite (from searchStoresByZip) to determine whether to mark this store as favorite
Current ZIP value from stores-search-form (used for fromZip when navigating to store_detail.html)
event_handlers: onViewDetailsClick: navigate to store_detail.html with id set to store.id and fromZip set to the current ZIP input value if defined.
onOrderPickupClick: navigate to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to store.id so the order flow starts for this store.
onCardClick (optional): treat a click on the card body as equivalent to onViewDetailsClick for quick navigation.

components.functionality: Displays a compact summary of the current order if one exists, including item count, subtotal, total, pickup store, and pickup time, based on getCurrentOrderSummary. Offers a shortcut to continue editing or reviewing the active order.
data_binding: Order (getCurrentOrderSummary response: hasActiveOrder, orderId, itemCount, subtotalAmount, totalAmount, pickupDateTime)
event_handlers: onInit: called by page-init-controller to set initial data from getCurrentOrderSummary.
onRefreshRequest: can be called by other components (e.g., after login or navigation) to re-fetch getCurrentOrderSummary and update the widget.
onClick: if hasActiveOrder is true and pickupStore.id is defined, navigate to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to pickupStore.id.

components.functionality: Provides user authentication flows directly on the Stores page, including login and signup forms. Manages error messaging and forwards successful account data to the rest of the page.
data_binding: AccountProfile-like account object from loginWithEmailPassword response (fullName, email, loyaltyPointsBalance, loyaltyTier, favoriteStore)
AccountDashboard-like object from signupAccount response (fullName, email, loyaltyPointsBalance, loyaltyTier, favoriteStore)
Form state: email, password, fullName, confirmPassword, acceptTerms
event_handlers: onLoginSubmit: call loginWithEmailPassword(email, password); on success, emit an onAccountLoginSuccess event with the account payload to page-init-controller and header-account-controls; on failure, display the error message in the panel.

onSignupSubmit: call signupAccount(fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, acceptTerms); on success, emit onAccountLoginSuccess with the accountDashboard context and update the UI to a logged-in state; on failure, display the error message.
onSwitchMode: toggle between login view and signup view within the panel without reloading the page.
onClose: hide the panel when the user dismisses it, maintaining current account state.

components.functionality: Shows account-related actions in the page header, such as 'Log In', 'Create Account', and 'Log Out', and displays basic account info when the user is authenticated.
data_binding: AccountProfile-like account object from account-auth-panel login/signup responses (for fullName, loyaltyTier, and favoriteStore display)
Authentication state flag derived from whether an account object is present
event_handlers: onClickLogin: open account-auth-panel in login mode.
onClickSignup: open account-auth-panel in signup mode.
onClickLogout: delegate to logout-button to perform logoutCurrentAccount and then clear displayed account info.
onAccountLoginSuccess: receive account context from account-auth-panel and render authenticated header state.
onAccountLogoutSuccess: reset header to logged-out state.

components.functionality: Executes the logout flow when a logged-in user chooses to sign out from the Stores page.
data_binding: Authentication state flag (whether a user is currently logged in)
event_handlers: onClick: call logoutCurrentAccount(); on success, emit onAccountLogoutSuccess to page-init-controller, header-account-controls, account-auth-panel, and stores-search-form so they can update their state and optionally refresh results.

components.functionality: Provides navigation to the Stores page from the site header and ensures the current zip parameter is preserved when relevant.
data_binding: Current URL param: zip (if any) to ensure navigation back to Stores preserves search context
event_handlers: onClick: navigate to stores.html, including the existing zip parameter if one is present, so that the stores page can restore the previous ZIP search via page-init-controller.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column grid with a centered content column constrained to a fixed max-width (~420–480px). Header uses a three-part horizontal layout (left, center, right) aligned to the same page margins.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a full-width canvas with a single, narrow central content column (max-width ~440–480px) that is horizontally centered. A thin, full-width header bar sits at the very top and uses a three-part layout (left: main navigation link to Stores; center: page label; right: account controls including logout). Below the header, the central column presents content in a strict top-to-bottom order: store search heading and form, then an optional compact order summary panel, then the vertically stacked results list (composed of individual store cards), and finally a compact account/auth panel at the very bottom. All of these elements align to the same central column with no sidebars or parallel columns.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a full-width canvas with a single, narrow central content column (max-width ~440–480px) that is horizontally centered. A thin, full-width header bar sits at the very top and uses a three-part layout (left: main navigation link to Stores; center: page label; right: account controls including logout). Below the header, the central column presents content in a strict top-to-bottom order: store search heading and form, then an optional compact order summary panel, then the vertically stacked results list (composed of individual store cards), and finally a compact account/auth panel at the very bottom. All of these elements align to the same central column with no sidebars or parallel columns.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header bar occupies a shallow band at the top (~10–15% of viewport height), leaving most of the vertical space for the main content column. The central column is given generous top and bottom padding so that the search form and first few results visually sit in the middle band of the screen with ample white space above and below. Within the column, the search form and the first few store-result cards take up most of the initial vertical attention, while the order summary and account-auth panel are more compact and sit lower in the flow with tighter vertical footprints. Inputs, cards, and buttons use nearly the full width of the column (about 80–100% of the column width) with consistent horizontal padding inside each component.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout maintains a low content density: large margins around the central column, comfortable vertical spacing between every stacked element (headings, form, cards, panels), and minimal text per section. Each store-result card is clean and uncluttered, showing only key details. The order summary and account-auth panel are concise, avoiding promotional clutter. There is ample whitespace between cards and panels so that the user sees only a small set of actionable choices at once, reducing cognitive load.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts at the top header: the eye first catches the left-aligned Stores link, then slides to the centered page label, then to the right-aligned account/logout controls. From there, the eye drops naturally down to the main column where a bold 'Find a store' or equivalent heading and the search form become the primary focus. After interacting with the search, the user’s gaze moves downward to the order summary panel (if present), then into the vertical stack of store-result cards where each card’s primary action (e.g., 'Start Order' or 'View Details') becomes the main focal point. At the bottom of the column, the smaller, less prominent account-auth panel provides a final, low-priority prompt to sign in or create an account.

layout.overall_description: The page presents a clean, modern, corporate-style layout with a thin white header bar stretching across the top of the screen. Inside that bar, content is centered to match a narrow max-width column below, creating a clear left-center-right structure: a subtle Stores navigation link on the left, a small page label like 'Find a Store' in the center, and concise account controls (including a logout button when relevant) on the right. Beneath this bar, the main experience is a single, vertically stacked column floating in the middle of a white page with generous margins on both sides. At the top of this column sits a prominent heading and the store search form: a labeled input and primary blue search button that span most of the column width. Directly below, a compact order summary panel uses the same card-like styling, giving a quick snapshot of the current order without overpowering the search or results. Under that, the search results appear as a vertical list of store cards, each a full-width, lightly bordered tile with store details and a clear action. Spacing between cards is generous, allowing each store to be scanned individually. At the very bottom of the column, a minimalist account-auth panel offers sign-in and sign-up options with smaller text and a single, modest CTA, visually signaling that authentication is helpful but not required. Throughout, the brand blue is reserved for key interactive elements and headings, with everything else in neutral tones, maintaining a calm, focused, and uncluttered browsing experience.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This controller component has no visual presence on the page. It does not occupy any explicit space in the layout and does not affect the position or size of other components. It sits logically at the top of the page’s structure, initializing state and data for the search form, results list, order summary, and auth panel, but the visual layout is entirely defined by the other components.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; no rendered box; does not participate in layout flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header-account-controls component forms a thin, full-width horizontal bar pinned to the very top of the page. It has a white background, subtle bottom border or shadow, and a height around 56–72px (about 10–15% of the initial viewport height). Inside this bar, content is constrained to the same centered max-width as the main column below. The internal layout is three-part: the left third anchors the header-stores-link aligned flush with the left edge of the content container, the center area holds a small, single-line page label (e.g., 'Find a Store') centered horizontally within the container, and the right third aligns account-related controls, including the logout-button, to the far right. All elements are inline and vertically centered within the bar. The bar remains visually light and unobtrusive so that the main vertical column below can dominate attention.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the top of the document flow; full-width horizontal strip that scrolls off-screen with the page (not sticky or fixed).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header-stores-link appears as a simple text-based navigation link positioned in the top header bar’s left section. It is aligned flush left within the centered header content container, sitting on the same horizontal baseline as the page label and logout button. The link uses the brand blue color but a modest font size, making it clearly clickable without visually competing with the main page content. It occupies minimal width—just enough for its text—leaving ample horizontal whitespace to its right before the center label begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline element within the header bar; its position is anchored to the left side of the header’s internal three-part layout and moves with the header when the page scrolls.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The logout-button lives inside the right section of the top header bar. It is right-aligned within the same centered content container, sitting at the far right edge, horizontally opposite the header-stores-link. Visually, it is a compact control—either a text button in brand blue or a small outlined pill—significantly smaller than primary CTAs in the main content area. Its height is just enough to feel tappable while aligning vertically with the header’s height, and its width is determined by its label plus comfortable horizontal padding. Surrounding whitespace ensures it doesn’t crowd the edge or any neighboring account elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline control positioned within the right side of the header bar; static and scrolls off-screen with the header.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The stores-search-form is the first and most prominent block in the main central column, positioned directly below the header bar with generous top margin (roughly 48–72px) so it feels anchored in the middle band of the viewport. It is centered horizontally and limited to a max-width of about 440–480px, occupying nearly the full width on small screens but never stretching too wide on larger ones. Within this block, content is arranged in a vertical stack: a bold, left-aligned heading (e.g., 'Find a store'), a short, lighter-weight instruction line beneath it, then the search controls. The primary search input (ZIP, city, or address) spans about 100% of the column width, with its label directly above, left-aligned. Below the input, a full-width primary blue button (e.g., 'Search') occupies nearly the entire column width, echoing the bold 'Sign In' button behavior from the mockup. Optional auxiliary controls (like 'Use my current location') appear as a smaller, single-line link or text below the primary button, center- or left-aligned and spaced a bit closer to the button than to the next section, reinforcing their lower priority. Vertical spacing between all these elements is consistent and generous, making the form feel light and approachable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Block-level element in the main central column; static and flows naturally after the header, pushing subsequent elements downward.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The order-summary-widget appears directly below the stores-search-form, separated by a moderate vertical gap (about 24–32px) so it feels like a related but secondary panel. It shares the same centered max-width as the search form and aligns perfectly with its left and right edges to maintain a single-column rhythm. Visually, it takes the form of a compact card: white background, subtle border or shadow, and slightly rounded corners, similar in style to a store-result card but slightly more compact. Inside, content is stacked vertically: a small, left-aligned title (e.g., 'Current Order'), a concise list or summary of items, and a subtle inline link or small button (e.g., 'View order') aligned to the right or placed beneath the summary. This panel’s height is modest—roughly 20–30% of the viewport height at most when populated—so it does not overshadow the search results that follow. Ample padding within the card and whitespace above and below the widget help it feel readable but clearly secondary to the search and results.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block within the main central column, vertically stacked immediately after the search form and before the results list.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The stores-results-list sits directly under the order-summary-widget, separated by an airy vertical gap (around 24px) that visually marks the transition from 'controls' to 'results'. It occupies the full width of the central column, matching the same max-width and horizontal alignment as the search form and order summary. The list renders as a vertical stack of store-result-card components, each card separated by consistent vertical spacing (about 16–24px). The first one or two cards will usually be visible without scrolling, giving immediate feedback after a search, while additional cards extend downward, making the entire column scrollable. There is no multi-column arrangement; cards never sit side-by-side. The list itself does not have a distinct card background; instead, it relies on the individual cards’ own styling and the surrounding whitespace to delineate items.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block that grows vertically as more results are shown; scrollable as part of the overall page content. It follows the order-summary-widget and precedes the account-auth-panel in the vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each store-result-card appears as a full-width tile within the central column, constrained to the same max-width and centered horizontally. The card has a white background, a light border or very subtle shadow, and small corner rounding, echoing the clean, flat aesthetic. Internally, content is arranged in a simple top-to-bottom flow: the store name at the top in a slightly larger, bold font; a second line with distance and key info (e.g., '0.5 miles • Open until 9 PM'); then the address and perhaps a short line for amenities or notes. At the bottom of the card, primary actions are laid out in a single row: a brand-blue primary button such as 'Start Order' aligned to the left or right, with secondary text links like 'View details' placed adjacent but visually lighter. Horizontal padding is generous on all sides, and vertical padding keeps the card content comfortable without feeling crowded. Each card’s height is flexible based on content but generally occupies 15–25% of the viewport height on typical screens. A consistent vertical gap between cards preserves the airy, low-density feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static list item within the stores-results-list; stacked vertically with other cards. Its width is dictated by the central column; it does not float or align side-by-side with other elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The account-auth-panel is positioned at the very bottom of the main central column, following the last store-result-card with a noticeable but not excessive vertical gap (around 32px). It shares the same centered max-width and aligns with all content above it, maintaining the strict single-column structure. Visually, it is understated: either a borderless block of text or a very lightly framed card. Inside, content mirrors the login mockup in a compact form: a small, friendly heading (e.g., 'Sign in to save your favorite stores'), a short explanatory sentence, a medium-width blue button (e.g., 'Sign In') that spans around 70–100% of the column width, and a low-priority inline text link below (e.g., 'Don’t have an account? Sign up'). The text is smaller than the main headings, and the vertical spacing between lines is tighter, clearly conveying that this section is supportive rather than primary to the current task of finding a store. Extra whitespace below the panel helps it feel like the natural visual endpoint of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block positioned at the end of the main column’s vertical flow; it scrolls into view after the results list and does not stick or float.

name: Store Detail
title: Store Details
description: Detail page for a single café location where users can view full store information, mark the store as their favorite (when logged in), and start or switch a pickup order from this store.

core_features: Load and display detailed information for a single store using getStoreDetail with the storeId taken from the id URL parameter.
Show address, contact information, hours, open/closed status, amenities, distance, and a map preview for the selected store based on the getStoreDetail response.

Allow logged-in users to set or update this store as their favorite by calling setFavoriteStore, with favorite state reflected using the isFavorite flag from getStoreDetail or the favoriteStore returned in the login/signup responses.
Provide a login and signup flow (using loginWithEmailPassword and signupAccount) directly on the page to enable favorite-store functionality and account-aware behavior.
Allow users to set this store as the current pickup store and start or switch a draft order by calling setCurrentPickupStore, then navigate to menu.html with pickupStoreId in the URL.
Display a compact current-order summary using getCurrentOrderSummary, including item count, amounts, and pickup store, and provide navigation to the menu page for editing the order.
Provide a contextual "Back to search results" link that navigates back to stores.html with the zip URL parameter derived from fromZip, when present.
Allow users to log out from this page using logoutCurrentAccount, clearing account-related UI state (such as favorite marker and account name) while leaving store details and order summary intact.
user_workflows: View store details from search results: Step 1: User arrives on store_detail.html with an id URL parameter (and optionally fromZip) after clicking a store in stores.html. Step 2: On page load, the controller component reads the id parameter. Step 3: The controller calls getStoreDetail with storeId equal to the id parameter and stores the returned store and isFavorite values in page state. Step 4: The controller calls getCurrentOrderSummary to fetch any active order context. Step 5: The UI renders store header, address, hours, amenities, map preview, and favorite status using the store data.

Set this store as favorite (logged-in user): Step 1: User is logged in via a previous session or by logging in through the page’s authentication panel (using loginWithEmailPassword). Step 2: The favorite toggle component determines whether the current store is already the favorite by checking either the isFavorite value from getStoreDetail or comparing the store.id to the account.favoriteStore.id from the login response. Step 3: User clicks the "Set as favorite" control. Step 4: The component calls setFavoriteStore with storeId equal to the current store.id. Step 5: On success, the component updates the account’s favoriteStore in in-memory account context using the favoriteStore returned by setFavoriteStore and sets the local isFavorite state to true so the UI reflects the change.

Attempt to set this store as favorite (not logged in): Step 1: User arrives on the store detail page without an authenticated account context. Step 2: User clicks the favorite control. Step 3: The favorite toggle component detects that there is no active account context and does not call setFavoriteStore. Step 4: The component triggers an auth-required handler that focuses or expands the login/sign-up panel. Step 5: User logs in using loginWithEmailPassword or creates an account using signupAccount. Step 6: On successful authentication, the page updates the account context and recomputes whether the current store is the favorite (either from the returned favoriteStore or by calling getStoreDetail again). Step 7: The user can click the favorite control again, which now calls setFavoriteStore successfully.

Start or switch a pickup order from this store: Step 1: User views the store detail and the page has loaded store.canMobileOrder from getStoreDetail. Step 2: The pickup-order component checks getCurrentOrderSummary results to see if there is an active order and which pickupStore it uses. Step 3: If the store supports mobile ordering, the "Order Pickup from this Store" button is enabled; otherwise it is disabled with explanatory messaging. Step 4: If there is an active order with a different pickup store, clicking the order button triggers a confirmation interaction to inform the user that the pickup store will change. Step 5: Upon confirmation, or when there is no conflicting order, the component calls setCurrentPickupStore with the current store.id. Step 6: On success, the component optionally refreshes order summary by calling getCurrentOrderSummary again and then navigates to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to the current store.id in the URL.

Use login and signup from the store detail page: Step 1: User opens the authentication panel on the store detail page. Step 2: For login, the user enters email and password and submits the form; the component calls loginWithEmailPassword with these values. Step 3: On success, the page updates account context with the returned account data and recomputes favorite status for the current store based on account.favoriteStore.id. Step 4: For signup, the user fills in fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, and acceptTerms; the component calls signupAccount. Step 5: On successful signup, the page updates account context using accountDashboard and again recomputes favorite status for the current store. Step 6: After authentication, favorite-related controls become fully active and can call setFavoriteStore.

Return to search results: Step 1: User visits store_detail.html with both id and fromZip URL parameters after performing a search in stores.html. Step 2: The back-link component reads fromZip from the URL. Step 3: The component renders a back-link control since fromZip is present. Step 4: When the user clicks the back-link, the page navigates to stores.html with zip set to the fromZip value. Step 5: If fromZip is not present in the URL, the back-link either does not render or navigates to stores.html without additional parameters.

View current order summary from the store detail page: Step 1: On page load, the controller calls getCurrentOrderSummary and stores the response in page state. Step 2: The current-order-summary widget displays whether there is an active order using hasActiveOrder, showing itemCount, subtotalAmount, totalAmount, pickupStore.name, and pickupDateTime when present. Step 3: If the user changes the pickup store by calling setCurrentPickupStore from the order button, the widget is refreshed by calling getCurrentOrderSummary again. Step 4: When the user clicks the order summary widget, the page navigates to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to the summary’s pickupStore.id, allowing the user to review or modify the order.

Log out from store detail: Step 1: While on the store detail page, the user opens the account menu component. Step 2: The user clicks a logout control. Step 3: The component calls logoutCurrentAccount with no parameters. Step 4: On success, the page clears in-memory account context and updates UI elements: favorite controls revert to an unauthenticated state, account name is cleared, and any account-specific elements that used AccountProfile data are reset. Step 5: Store details and current order summary remain visible; any attempts to set a favorite store now prompt the login/sign-up panel again.
interactions: On page load, read id and fromZip URL parameters, validate the presence of id, call getStoreDetail with storeId derived from id, and call getCurrentOrderSummary to initialize store and order context.

Clicking the "Back to search results" link navigates to stores.html with the zip URL parameter set to the current page’s fromZip value when it exists; when fromZip is absent, the link navigates to stores.html without additional parameters or is hidden.

Clicking the favorite toggle when logged in calls setFavoriteStore with storeId equal to the current store.id; on a successful response, the UI updates the favorite indicator and account context using the returned favoriteStore and displays any success message.
Clicking the favorite toggle when not logged in does not call setFavoriteStore; instead it triggers a handler that opens or focuses the authentication panel so the user can log in or sign up.

Submitting the login form calls loginWithEmailPassword with the entered email and password; if success is true, the account context is updated and favorite status is recomputed for the current store; if success is false, the error message is displayed via the notification component and no favorite actions are enabled.

Submitting the signup form calls signupAccount with fullName, email, password, confirmPassword, and acceptTerms; if success is true, the accountDashboard data is used to populate account context and favorite status is recomputed; otherwise, the returned message is shown in an error state.

Clicking the "Order Pickup from this Store" button when store.canMobileOrder is true calls setCurrentPickupStore with this storeId, optionally after confirming a store switch if getCurrentOrderSummary indicates an active order at a different store; on success, the page navigates to menu.html with pickupStoreId set to the current store.id.

Clicking the "Order Pickup from this Store" button when store.canMobileOrder is false does not call setCurrentPickupStore and instead surfaces a non-interactive explanation that mobile ordering is not available for this location.

Clicking the current-order summary widget (when hasActiveOrder is true) navigates to menu.html with pickupStoreId equal to the pickupStore.id returned by getCurrentOrderSummary, allowing the user to modify the existing order.

Clicking the logout control in the account menu calls logoutCurrentAccount; on success, the page clears account-related state and disables or reconfigures controls that require authentication (such as favorite-store actions), while leaving store and order data already loaded in memory.
Dismissing an error or success message in the notification banner clears the currently displayed message from view without affecting underlying store, account, or order state.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page relies on URL parameters and in-memory state (not localStorage) to manage its behavior. On initialization, the page reads the id URL parameter; if id is present, getStoreDetail is called with storeId equal to id, and the resulting store object and isFavorite flag form the core store state. If id is missing or invalid, the page does not call getStoreDetail and instead can route the user back to stores.html or display an error state, controlled purely by navigation and in-memory flags. The fromZip URL parameter is read once at load and used to determine whether to show a "Back to search results" link; if present, clicking the back-link navigates to stores.html with zip set to the fromZip value.
